Now I can see:
- ID=6: N (light green) at [[30, 15], [30, 17], [32, 17], [32, 15]] with child ID=8 (W, white pixel)
- ID=10: c (charcoal) at [[32, 11], [32, 14], [33, 15], [36, 15], [36, 11]] - this is the left charcoal frame, but it has 15 pixels instead of 16, and the boundary is slightly different. It seems like the green object (ID=6) is now above the left charcoal frame (ID=10).
The green object with the white pixel is now at row 30-32, col 15-17. The left charcoal frame is at row 32-36, col 11-15. The green object is now above the charcoal frame.
So the green object moved up too far. I need to move it DOWN to get back inside the charcoal frame.
Let me try pressing DOWN to move the green object down to get back inside the charcoal frame.
